I wish I had access to that.
I have to do it.

I'm going to get you a real cloud system here pretty soon.
I'm sorry?
How are things going?
Pretty good.

It's doing pretty well.

So I know we were talking API and that's baked into kind of a bunch of our workflows.
So we have our own system that, you know, it's basically querying SEMrush and using their API to pull back like, you know, competitors.
It's pulling back keyword data, search volume, keyword difficulty, things like that.
So I was able to get Terry kind of the last, you know, month or so of our usage with the API.
And I just wanted to make sure that that doesn't raise any flags on your end.
Yeah, I'd love to.
I didn't see the total usage thing in there, but tell me a little bit more.
So this is even your tracking in SEMrush that you're pulling out all this data for?
No, so it's when we start writing.
Content.

So we'll pick the topic or keyword, and then our workflow will go and research all that for us, and all the keywords, it will pull the keyword difficulty, search volume, put together a list of keywords based off of all that data and competition, and then say, okay, here's the brief with all the keywords and things that we want to put into this article, and then it goes and writes it.
Oh, cool, cool, cool.
That was like $167,000 monthly, right?
Is that right, Dave?
Yeah, it's up there.
Yeah.
Yeah, though, why would you do that if Content Fusion already does the whole process and gives you the recommended keywords to use?
Yeah, that's how our current workflow...
That's the current workflow.
Yeah.
Okay.
I'd love to, if you have details on the API being used, I can make sure that we map those APIs and provide you an equivalent there.
Okay.
I can go in...
And I'll talk to our end, Jaan.
Yeah, let's make sure we circle that because that sounds like a critical workflow for you.
So I'm glad that that came up.

Well, even if, and this is the point I was trying to make to leadership was like, even if we just keep one account with SEMrush for the API usage, it's still cheaper than what they're trying to upgrade us to.

So one of the things, Terry, that they asked me to do was put a list of like a comparison table of, I hate when they make me do stuff like this, but it's a comparison table of SEMrush, their price, and what we're currently paying now.
I think you have the details on that.
And like what you are comparable offering from, you know, Clarity and, you know, the price there.
So they just want to see all the things in detail.
Yeah.
So is that something that you can help me with?
Yeah, absolutely.
can be super easy because you get so much more with what we're talking about here.

We have their business plan right now.

So we're paying just over, you know.
72K, yeah, right.
72K, yeah.

this is not the enterprise.
They were pitching you the enterprise, but this is not the enterprise.
This is the good old-fashioned SEMrush business.
Right, and then the problem with enterprise is the same thing.

Yeah.
Yeah.
A bunch But the guests users.
Users don't get enterprise access, they get regular access.
So it doesn't make any sense.
I did not know that.
Guest users get regular access.

No.
Yeah.

Mark this down.
Mark this date down.

You're using them for the keywords data.
It seems the API workflows, right?
Yeah.
So, yes, we are tracking keywords.

Basic.

So basic SEO tracking for them.
Um, I'd love, so...
Since I'm a power user, I know how we can build cohorts and we can build, you know, using RegEx and kind of build out and track, you know, by cohort or by, you know, topic or whatever.
So I'd love to build up types of views and build dashboards because currently I'm doing it through Looker and I'm getting like all sorts of sampling.
And then folks like my, you know, EME and my directors, they don't have like, you know, SQL or, you know, they can't write RegEx.
So the only way to avoid the sampling that I'm getting in Looker is to actually just mainly put a filter in using RegEx, but that sucks because like you, then it doesn't automatically update it.
So I'd rather just, you know, through Clarity build a way just to do it, you know, where like you're ingesting.
We just bring in the data, we bring it all unsampled and then just run it, save it, save it once, right?
Save a content type, save a, keep it intent once and then just use it wherever.
Yeah, that's exactly it.
We can outline like a dozen things that you get with Clarity that just doesn't exist in your SEM.
Exactly that kind of stuff, right?
Content types, cohorts, right?
Search intent cohorts, customized dashboards.

I think we'll have, that'll be a definite big win for us if you can show just how, like, you know, you're ingesting the data through the API, then you're warehousing it, and then you're avoiding sampling.

Because that's the main concern, like, I had a bunch of, we had a director meeting today, and they were talking about, like, you know, I created kind of a more advanced dashboard in Looker, and they're like, this is too much for us to demo with clients.
So I was like, okay, you know, let's put together a simple dashboard.
But if we could just build that through Clarity, and then they can have access and look at it and see it at any time without having to see sample data or any other stuff, that would be awesome.
Awesome.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So we already, I think we have half a dozen already here, you know, pre-built simple dashboards that are client ready.
Cohort analysis by content type, cohort analysis by search intent, you know, the alerting capabilities in the platform, API access to all of this data for your workflows.
And we'll keep that separate API that you're doing for the current workflow.
I do want to evaluate that, but I want to make sure that we totally understand and can support.
We have the API.
We just want to make sure it aligns with the way you use it.
Your API calls are different.
So if you send me over those API calls, too, I love that idea.
If you have the ability to just keep one small account running with business just to keep that API running, $350, maybe that's not worth all the retooling effort.
But at some point, we do want all of that, too, to come over so that there's no dependency on SEO.
Right.
Agreed.
But the fastest path is absolutely to keep that tooling in place and just move all this reporting stuff over.
yeah, ideally, we move quick.
Right.
So we.
Right.
Move it over, and we just keep one active, and then from there, we can move.
So I'll see.
The only other hiccup I see, and I didn't know about this one, so apparently one of our investors is on the board for SEMrush, so that could potentially be problematic for this.
Yeah, but they're not giving us any deals, and they're not doing anything out of their way.
So they're a quiet board member.
They shouldn't talk till they bring some business.
Yeah, so they're a board member, but they're not speaking for us and not trying to get us a deal.
They're not doing anything to help us out there.

So I don't see that being – that could potentially be a hiccup down the road, but I don't – you know, right now they're not doing anything to try and sway us, and they keep cutting out access to the platform too.
So they have an unusual, like, suspicious activity thing, and because we have so many folks logging into the account, don't they – flag it, we're out of work.
for, you know, an hour or two until we reset the password.
Oh, right, because they want to charge for every user.
So if two people log in from different IPs, they're like, uh-uh.
Yeah.
Oh, I hate that approach of this, but good for us.
You don't have any limits for users, right?
That should be, go on to the main list of things, unlimited users on your site.
Now, your clients would get a dashboard access, right?
Even with an SEM rush, or you would just give them dashboard access, not necessarily the whole platform, right?
Yeah, so I give them Looker dashboard access, but I'd rather give them clarity and just, you know.
Exactly.
We'll just give them read-only access to the dashboard, and that's it.
So the platform is for you to use, dashboards they can use.
They can review it with your comments and stuff and all that.
Yeah, that's fine.
Okay.
Cool.
So, Terry, let me know, like, if you can put together that kind of, if you, that comment.
Yeah, absolutely.
Okay.
And just so we're doing apples to apples, right, because we were talking about, doing a package with 25.
Hey, keywords, right?
Is that comparable to what the SEMrush current plan is?
I believe...
that as...
Should we mention that as a differentiator?
Yeah, let me double check to see what we have for keywords.
Yeah, that'd be cool to be able to say, like, you're getting 2x the number of search queries with us, right?
Yeah.
Or 2x the number of projects, whatever the terminology is.
We'll match the terminology.
Yep, give me one second and I'll log in right here.
Okay, so give me one second to log in and then I'll...
Yeah, sure.
Subscription info, right?
Yeah, so it says...
We have 2,312 keywords.
Oh my god, you're getting 10x the number of search queries now.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That's there, right there.
Yeah, that's correct, yeah.
Right?
Should we call that out or are they going to go like, well, do we need those money?
Well, yeah, you need that money.
We agree, but I'm just anticipating a question, right?
Yeah.
That's what I see there.
Let me just double check.
Perfect.
Then API usage, there's two, it keeps, we use so much that it breaks their web front-end, so I can't load it.
That's incredible.
They're charging you that much for 2,300 queries.
That's a lot of unseen, no?
Yeah, so it's that plus all the users.
we have like 40 users.
That's where a lot of it's coming from for sure, yeah.
You're paying four grand a month.
That's for users to access this.
I know.
Yeah.
Okay.
Even with the 20 You should connect me to your CEO, Dave.
I just want to be aghast.
I just want to talk to your CEO and be aghast.
Do you understand what you're doing?
I know.
I know.
So it is – so I can only raise awareness and I can only tell them what they're doing.
Yeah.
We're like at 46 out of 50 users on the business plan, right?
So I can only raise awareness and I can only tell them and I can say, this is a  deal.
Like you're getting – basically you're getting screwed over and like I can save you a lot of money if we want this route and give you a better product.
But, you know, I can – support and everything that you don't get right now, right?
Customers, client success, technical support, everything.
You need to just make sure that we offload some of the work over to us, right?
Yeah.
Do we have any
Dev hours or any engineering hours that you can give us in that package?
Whatever you need, whatever you need, Dave, just shoot me an email and tell me what you need, we'll build it.
Okay.
I mean, even still at 6K a month with our 25K keyword package, we're still cheaper.

So we'll try to be as in your face and bring awareness to that in the comparison chart that we'll do, which will hopefully help your case and obviously give you some ammo to work with and have your back on that for sure.
But yeah, let's, Mitul and I'll, I'll put it together and Mitul will ask for your feedback on it once I kind of put it together and we can take a look at it before we send it over to Dave for his.
So I had another item, right, is product roadmap input, right?
Something to the effect of like you are part of our team to help us navigate this product development.
So if you need something, you're part of our, you're going to have.
Product roadmap input into whatever we built.
Yeah, I mean, I've submitted some requests to get some stuff built before in the past, and you've all kind of evaluated and built things for me.

So definitely, I guess if maybe there's a just a comparison, like maybe if you can send a comparison dashboard or just like a dashboard for what a partner would look like, maybe the H&M account and just like a standard.
That'll be helpful, too, so they can see, oh, wow, like, you know, reporting out of the box.
Like we can go in and, you know, customize the report.
There's all the widgets and all the things.
Yeah, we have a great sample dashboard with a variety of widgets and stuff that we can throw in there.
Yeah, that'll be helpful, too.
But again, I can only raise awareness.
can only show them, you know, and I can lead a horse to water, but I can't drink.
We'll all try to push the head down a little bit more.
Don't worry, I've been around horses for a long time.
They typically, you push their head down like.
You're getting tossed.
All right.
The water may be done.
That might work, yes.

I think this is fantastic.
Okay.
Very helpful.
All righty.

Okay.
Go from there.
All right.

Yeah.

Okay.
Perfect.

Okay.
We'll make this work.
All fine.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss GrowthX.ai's current SEO tool usage and potential transition to seoClarity platform.](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX.ai currently uses SEMrush's business plan (\~$72K/year) for keyword tracking and API usage, but faces limitations and high costs](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)
  - [seoClarity offers a more comprehensive solution with 10x more keywords (25K vs 2.3K), unlimited users, and custom reporting capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)
  - [GrowthX.ai's leadership requires a detailed comparison between SEMrush and seoClarity offerings to consider the switch](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Topics

### Current SEO Tool Usage

  - [GrowthX.ai uses SEMrush business plan, paying \~$5-6K/month ($72K/year)](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
  - [Tracking \~2,312 keywords](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=712.0)
  - [Heavy API usage for content creation workflow (costing \~$167K monthly)](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=77.0)
  - [46 out of 50 user limit reached, causing login issues and extra costs](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)

### seoClarity Advantages

  - [Offering 25K keywords (10x more than current SEMrush plan)](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0)
  - [Unlimited users vs. SEMrush's per-user charging model](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=633.0)
  - [Custom dashboard creation without sampling issues](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
  - [Cohort analysis by content type and search intent](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [API access for existing workflows](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=70.0)
  - [Potential for engineering support and product roadmap input](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)

### Transition Considerations

  - [Keep one SEMrush account ($350/month) for API usage during transition](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=513.0)
  - [Potential conflict: GrowthX.ai investor on SEMrush board (not actively helping)](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
  - [Need for detailed comparison table: SEMrush vs. seoClarity offerings and pricing](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=235.0)

### Reporting and Dashboard Requirements

  - [Current use of Looker for client dashboards, facing sampling issues](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [Need for customizable, client-ready dashboards without data sampling](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)
  - [Request for sample dashboard showcasing out-of-the-box reporting capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=910.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Terry to create detailed comparison table of SEMrush vs. seoClarity offerings](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=652.0)
  - [Dave to obtain and share API usage details from GrowthX.ai engineers](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=701.0)
  - [seoClarity team to provide sample dashboard for client reporting](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=930.0)
  - [Dave to compile information into document for leadership review](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=983.0)
  - [Explore potential for including engineering hours in seoClarity package](https://fathom.video/share/LZ1hrKx1Auos7e43FKxH57QKTub29zS6?tab=summary&timestamp=843.0)

action_items.description: Create SEMrush vs seoClarity comparison table (pricing, features, 25K keywords, unlimited users)
Review Terry's comparison table before sending to Dave
Prepare sample seoClarity dashboard (H&M account or similar) showing out-of-box reporting
Create Ocean doc for leadership w/ SEMrush vs seoClarity comparison (after receiving from Terry)
Contact engineer for SEMrush API usage details; send to Terry/Mitul
